title: Boat Rental in Rostock: A 2026 Guide to Sailing the Baltic Coast
html:
Rostock, on the Baltic Sea, is a gateway to the Warnow river and the open waters of the Bay of Mecklenburg. For a half-day motorboat from the Stadthafen, expect to pay from €250, and you can rent a boat through BoatHire24, the worldwide marketplace for boat hire, to compare verified listings and book your trip.

Where to set off in Rostock?

The Stadthafen in the city centre is the main departure point, with pontoons and chandlery facilities. Yachthafen Hohe Düne in Warnemünde offers deeper berths and direct access to the Baltic. Bootshafen Markgrafenheide is quieter and closer to the Darss-Zingst peninsula. For a day sail, head to the island of Poel (12 nautical miles) or Kühlungsborn (15 nautical miles). The Warnow estuary itself is sheltered and perfect for beginners.

Which boat to rent?

Explore the options available on BoatHire24, with prices starting from around €150 for a small motorboat.

  1. Motorboat (25, 30ft), Best for a half-day cruise on the Warnow; seats 4, 6; from €200 for four hours.
  2. Sailing yacht (30, 36ft), Ideal for a full-day Baltic outing; sleeps 4, 6; from €350 for eight hours.
  3. Rib (rigid inflatable boat), Fast and nimble for exploring the shallow bays; seats 4; from €180 per half-day.
  4. Catamaran (40, 45ft), Stable and spacious for groups or families; sleeps 6, 8; from €600 for a day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. the Princess F70), Crewed charter with onboard amenities; up to 10 guests; from €1,200 per day. For details, see the Princess F70 for hire.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Rostock?

Prices vary from €50 per person for a shared tour to over €1,500 for a crewed yacht.

  • Shared sailing tour: from €50 per person (2, 3 hours, includes skipper).
  • Small motorboat (half-day, self-drive): from €200 for 4 hours.
  • Catamaran (full-day, bareboat): from €600 for 8 hours.
  • Luxury crewed motor yacht (full-day): from €1,200 for up to 10 guests.

To compare these and other verified options, you can rent a boat in Rostock through the platform and see up-to-date prices.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Rostock?

For motorboats under 15 hp or 4.5 metres, no licence is required; larger vessels typically need a German Sportbootführerschein Binnen or See. Rental operators on BoatHire24 confirm the exact licence requirements for each boat before booking. Many skippers are available if you prefer a crewed charter.

Local tip: In July and August, the prevailing north-westerly wind off Warnemünde can pick up in the afternoon, so plan your return before 4pm if you are a novice.

When is the best season for boating in Rostock?

The peak season runs from May to September, with warmest waters and longest daylight in July and August. June and September offer milder crowds and still-good sailing conditions. The high season also sees the Warnemünde Week regatta in early July, a major Baltic sailing event.

At a glance

  • Price range: €50, €1,500 per trip
  • Peak months: May to September
  • Typical trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Max capacity: up to 10 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Rostock

Can I anchor for a swim at Poel island?

Yes, the south coast of Poel has shallow, sandy bays suitable for anchoring, but check local by-laws first.

Are there any rental restrictions for non-EU licence holders?

International licence holders should obtain a translation or an ICC to comply with German regulations.

Is it possible to rent a boat for a week in Rostock?

Weekly charters are available, especially for yachts and catamarans, with discounts often applied for seven-day hires.

Can I bring my dog on a boat rental?

Many operators allow dogs, but you must confirm at booking and expect a small cleaning fee.

Book a boat in Rostock through BoatHire24 for your Baltic adventure, or check availability across hundreds of destinations. Boat owners can also list their craft to reach a global audience and earn from their vessel. To understand how to get started, see how boat rental works on the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Rostock and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
